content.explanation:
📝 How Text Works in HTML

In HTML, text does NOT automatically go to the next line when you press Enter.

Instead, the browser treats text as continuous unless you tell it otherwise using tags.

📦 Paragraphs (

)

The <p> tag is used to create separate blocks of text.

Each paragraph automatically gets spacing above and below it.

🧠 Simple Rule

👉 Use <p> for full text blocks
👉 Use line breaks only when you need small breaks inside text
content.syntax_breakdown:
🔧 Line Breaks (
)

The <br> tag forces text to move to the next line instantly.

It does NOT create a new paragraph — it just breaks the line.

⚡ Key Difference

  • 📦 <p> = full block with spacing
  • ➡️ <br> = simple line jump inside text

🧪 Example

<p>
First line<br>
Second line<br>
Third line
</p>

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Text Layouts</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Organize this text -->
    Cymor Code Learner Hub
    Learning HTML from zero to advanced.
    Build and host modern websites.
</body>
</html>

quiz_engine.explanation_feedback:
Correct 🎉

takeaways: HTML does not automatically respect line breaks from your editor
creates full text paragraphs with spacing
creates a simple line break inside text
does not require a closing tag

cheat_sheet.p: Used for full paragraphs of text
cheat_sheet.br: Used for simple line breaks inside text
